返回日报
Hacker News #11

欧洲数字身份钱包依赖谷歌和苹果的安全服务

European digital ID wallets rely on safety services of Google and Apple

科技产品 作者 donohoe 热度 706 回复 306 2026/06/30 18:36
打开原帖 打开外部链接 JSON

摘要

  • European governments are rolling out digital identity wallets, which are to be used by citizens to access services, and to verify their age online. As reported by Follow the Money and Android Authority, there is a serious problem with this: these wallets rely on safety services of Google and Apple. These are known as Google Play Integrity API, and Apple’s Managed Device Attestation1. Such safety services (known as “remote attestation”) are used to ensure that wallet apps run on hardware that is not tampered with. In this article we explain why the EU-wallet case is part of a bigger problem: by embedding these safety services in public infrastructure, Europe risks making society dependent on private companies while serving their corporate interests.Here is the problem:Google’s Play Integrity API is not just a security feature: it is reinforcing Google’s control over the Android ecosystem.Google’s Play Integrity API is an instructive case for how big tech platform companies accrue power. The API is a free piece of software that Google gifts to developers to help with their app development. It allows developers to check whether an app is running on a “genuine certified Android device” to test the integrity of a mobile device. This can help developers reduce abuse by bots, fraud in banking apps, or cheating in game apps. But in doing so, it also checks whether a device is running a Google-licensed version of Android and treats unlicensed alternatives as a potential security risk. When Google verifies whether an app has been tampered with, it uses the Google Play Store as the source of truth, checking both whether the app has been modified and whether it was installed through the Play Store. As a result, Google’s safety service is designed to exclude operating systems that are not licensed by Google, encourage installation through the Google Play Store, and require users to sign in with a Google account. This is a clear violation of the Digital Market Act (DMA).We do have a choice. A more open alternative to Google Play Integrity exists but is being ignored: Android's Hardware Attestation API. It provides hardware-based security checks but without enforcing Google’s ecosystem policy. Governments are cementing a monopoly they claim to opposeThe EU often states that it wants to break the big tech monopoly. Yet, European member states risk reinforcing Google's ecosystem when they embed the Google Play Integrity API into their digital ID wallet architecture. For example, wallet developers in the Netherlands and Italy have implemented Play Integrity. As a result, users of de-Googled operating systems such as e/OS and GrapheneOS can be excluded from accessing these services.In this way, governments effectively become enforcers of a private company's platform policies. This stands in tension with Europe's ambition to build digital public infrastructure based on public values such as openness, inclusiveness, and technological sovereignty. It also stands in tension with the regulation underpinning the EU’s identity wallet, which identifies interoperability as a key objective. Users who want the autonomy to use operating systems without pre-installed Google software, Google trackers, and built-in LLMs, are forced to use Google software, if they want to use the wallet. And here, they will not have a choice.ID wallets are public infrastructure to access critical public services. They should remain interoperable across different devices and operating systems, free from vendor lock-in.ID wallets are not just any kind of software – they are key means to access government documents and manage logins to public services. Therefore, they are often seen as crucial building blocks of digital public infrastructure. They are a crucial service that has to be available to anyone - independently of Google and Apple. Because the consequence is that alternative de-Googled operating systems are much less attractive to adopt if users cannot use crucial apps like identity wallets to log into government services. Waag’s own research findings on this topic support this. In the EU-funded Mobifree project, we have researched over the past two years what makes de-Googled mobile software ecosystems valuable for different end-users. A key requirement for many of our 120 testers to switch to de-Googled operating systems was their compatibility with apps for critical services such as payments and government identification apps.Government developers therefore have to consider deeper stack levels when optimizing interoperability. Since Play Integrity API clearly violates the Digital Markets Act, it also contradicts the goals of ID wallets to advance European sovereignty. European member states lack a unified approach to implement wallets Part of the problem lies in the governance of the wallet design process. The EU provides a general technical framework for the wallet architecture, the Architecture Reference Framework. While it does not require European governments to use Google attestation, it does recommend it. This leads to an incoherent European stance towards Google, with some countries not using it, while others enforce Google’s ecosystem. Some member states, such as Italy, have interpreted the EU’s recommendation to use the Play Integrity API as mandatory. Others, like Switzerland, rely on Android’s attestation mechanism. They dropped Play Integrity due to data protection, data sovereignty, and freedom-of-choice concerns. The Netherlands and Italy use Play Integrity unconditionally. By doing so, they interpret the EU’s recommendations for using Google’s and Apple’s attestation software in very strict terms. If Europe is serious about digital autonomy, it should rule out Google and Apple attestation entirely from the Architecture Reference Framework and mandate open, hardware-based attestation mechanisms. Countries like Switzerland demonstrate that using Google Play Integrity is not justified, and that other solutions are available. Public infrastructure demands public accountability, and there are ways to actBecause digital wallets are public infrastructure, their design must be subject to public participation and accountability. The problems and contradictions explained above deserve a public debate. Citizens and developers are raising concerns on national repositories — including Germany's public wallet development tracker (gitlab.opencode.de) and Switzerland's open discussion forum (github.com/orgs/swiyu-admin-ch). These are legitimate channels, but they reach only a narrow technical audience. If you are an expert working on this topic who wants to promote change, get in touch. What you can do:If you are a user of alternative, de-Googled operating systems, contact the developers of your country's EUDI Wallet app and demand independence from Google and Apple attestation (for the Dutch wallet, go to the contact page of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' EDI website)If you are a concerned citizen, contact your elected representatives to demand making ID wallets independent from Google and Apple. If you are a journalist: follow the political and design process. Like the recent Dutch Solvinity case, this story deserves ongoing and wide coverage because it may be a watershed moment to cement Google’s and Apple’s power position, or not. See the EUDI Wallet webpage on developer.overheid.nl for development updates and repositories, and check out the EDI website of the Dutch Ministry of Foreign Affairs for meetups and contact details.NotesIn this article, we focus on Google’s Play Integrity API. We do so because it has an impact on the use of alternative operating systems based on Android.

命中 4 个 AI/科技关键词。

Luker88 2026/06/30 19:30
欧盟钱包参考严格要求 Google Play 服务 https://github.com/eu-digital-identity-wallet/eudi-app-andro...

所以意大利的IO应用程序 https://github.com/pagopa/io-app （钱包、文件、年龄验证）不断拒绝用户对GrapheneOS支持的请求并要求谷歌。

在诉讼开始之前，一切都不会改变。

唯一的希望是 motorola/grapheneOS 的合作和消费者协会，他们可能会起诉反竞争行为。

在任何需要播放服务的应用程序频道上发出声音，如果诉讼开始，这将在未来有所帮助，因为这将表明用户对该倡议的支持。

The EU reference for wallets strictly required google play services https://github.com/eu-digital-identity-wallet/eudi-app-andro...

So Italy's IO app https://github.com/pagopa/io-app (wallet, documents, age verification) continuously refuses the users' request for GrapheneOS support and requires google.

Nothing will change until the lawsuits start coming in.

The only hope is the motorola/grapheneOS collaboration and consumer associations, that might sue for anticompetitive behavior.

Make noise on any channel for the apps that require play services, it will help in the future if the lawsuits start, since it will show user support for the initiative.

WhyNotHugo 2026/06/30 20:15
问题不仅仅是技术依赖性。

事实上，它迫使每个公民向公司支付数百欧元，而这些公司随后又侵犯了他们的权利。

如果出现问题，公民得不到任何形式的支持，并且必须签订一份极其不对称的合同协议，该公司几乎不承担任何责任，但拥有非常充分的权利以极其令人毛骨悚然的方式追踪另一方。

The issue isn't just the technical dependency.

It's also the fact that it forces each citizen to pay a few hundred Euros to companies which then campaign against their very rights.

Citizens get no support of any kind in case of issues, and has to enter a contractual agreement which is ridiculously asymmetrical, where the company has little to no responsibility of any kind, but has very ample rights to track the other party in extremely creepy ways.

楼层
microtonal 2026/06/30 22:10
我认为有两场战斗都值得打：

1. 完全禁止远程认证。

2. 在提供远程认证的世界中，让它以公平的方式控制，而不仅仅是由谷歌和苹果控制。

风险在于，只争取（1）会让你进入一个远程认证的世界，只有谷歌和苹果才能决定谁能通过，谁不能通过。事实上，这几乎就是我们现在所处的世界。

我同意它们都值得为之奋斗，但我认为 (2) 更容易实现，因为 Play Integrity 可能违反了 DMA。 （IANAL 等等）

I think there are two fights that are both worth fighting:

1. Completely outlawing remote attestation.

2. In a world where remote attestation is given, let it be controlled in a fair way and not just by Google and Apple.

The risk is that only fighting for (1) leaves you in a world with remote attestation, where only Google and Apple can decide who gets to pass and who not. In fact, that is pretty much the world we are in already.

I agree that they are both worth fighting for, but I think (2) is much easier to accomplish, simply because Play Integrity is probably a DMA violation. (IANAL blah blah)

楼层
altairprime 2026/07/01 08:09
实施#2就像声明颁发证明根的硬件供应商不得在其上预装操作系统一样简单，也不得制定排他性或不公平的定价协议，从而对其他企业产生不均匀的优势或劣势；并且，必须提供一种方式让消费者随意更换第三方预装的操作系统。如果欧盟拥有技术能力，那么他们将努力实现这一目标，并在一项法令中彻底打破苹果/谷歌的垄断，因为这些欧盟销售的设备将可以在全球范围内重新利用（如果他们选择实施的话，可以为替代操作系统提供有效的证明链）。一个人可以梦想。
Implementing #2 would be as simple as declaring that hardware vendors that issue attestation roots may not pre-install operating systems on them, nor make exclusivity or unfair pricing agreements that advantage or disadvantage other businesses unevenly; and, must provide a way for consumers to replace at will the operating system preinstalled by those third parties with one of their own choosing. If the EU has technical competence then they’ll work their way around to this realization and absolutely shatter the Apple/Google monopolies in a single edict, since those EU-sold devices will then be repurposeable worldwide (with valid attestation chains to the replacement OSes, if they choose to implement them). One can dream.

jt2190 2026/06/30 22:45
为什么认证总是不好？当两个人互动时，双方都会进行信任/风险计算。证明不就是降低双方风险的一种手段吗？ （我们可以争论谁应该控制证明过程以及它应该如何工作，但你的观点 1 表明永远不存在好的证明形式。）我们会做什么呢？
Why is attestation always bad, all the time? When two people interact there’s a trust/risk calculation on both sides. Isn’t attestation just a means of reducing risk for both parties? (We can debate who should control the attestation process and how it should work but your point 1 suggests that there is never a good form of attestation.) What would we do instead?

femboyvtuber 2026/06/30 23:07
我想不出有哪家公司适当地使用证明作为信任/风险计算。我在各大游戏工作室工作；从来没有计算只有二进制。

它从不“让我们检查移动用户是否在服务器端购买了游戏内内容以防止盗版”，它“暂停任何使用安全网失败的设备登录的帐户，永久禁止任何未通过越狱或根检查的帐户”

它永远不会“让我们检查和计算统计作弊概率并将损害计算移动到服务器端，以便玩家无法上帝模式或修改他们的APK”，它的“所有非库存手机都是作弊者和欺诈者，禁止所有这些，使用侵入性反作弊，同时继续拥有客户端损害以及健康和能量，因为它更容易”

其他一些事情必须首先改变，否则商业的唯一选择将是，在 2 实施后：“虽然现在可以允许中立的第三方控制证明，但法律等高层人士表示只有谷歌可以，我们将禁止其他所有人”

只要不关心更容易，那就是我们会采取的选择。 z.B.我们的发行商允许删除游戏服务的唯一原因是发现中国玩家在绝对不是谷歌认证的手机上花费最多的数量级，即使如此，它也只是放宽了对特定地区的检查，迫使所有欧盟玩家继续进行这项检查。

如果结果是继续需要证明但允许 GrapheneOS f.e.，我完全不会感到惊讶。仅在摩托罗拉工厂发货的手机中，如果用户以任何方式参与安装，则不允许这样做。

I cannot think of any company that has appropriately used attestation as a trust/risk calculation. I work in major game studio; there is never calculation only a binary.

It never „let`s check if the mobile user has purchased in-game content server side to prevent pirating it“, its „suspend any account that has signed in with a device that fails safetynet, permanently ban any account that has failed a jailbreak or root checks“

It never „let`s check and calculate statistically cheating probability and move damage calculation server-side so that player cannot godmode or modify their APK“, its „all non-stock phones are cheaters and fraudsters, ban all of them, use invasive anti-cheat, while continuing to have client sided damage and health and energy because it is easier“

Something else has to change first otherwise the only option for businsinses do will be, after 2 is implemented : „while yes it is now possible to allow a neutral third party to control attestation, someone higher-up such as legal has said ONLY google can and we will ban everyone else“

As long as it is easier to don't give a fuck, that is the option that will be taken. z.B. the only reason our publisher allow the removal of play services was finding out that chinese players on definitely not google certified phone spends the most by orders of magnitude and even then it is only relaxing the check for specific region, forcing all EU players to continue to have this checking.

I would be wholly unsurprised if the result was to continue to require attestation but allow GrapheneOS f.e. only in Motorola factory shiped phones and disallow it if the user was involved in any way in the installation of it.

qurren 2026/07/01 02:07
> 当两个人互动时，双方都会进行信任/风险计算。

您绝不应该将信任建立在对方所拥有的硬件具有您认可的限制的基础上。这是脆弱的，尤其是在一个有些人比其他人更擅长制造或修改硬件的世界里。阻止人们修改自己拥有的硬件也是对基本自由的根本侵犯，而不是您可以可靠监管的硬件，因此从技术角度来看，这是建立信任的糟糕方法。

最好将信任建立在协议级别上已建立的加密方法上。您将它们视为黑匣子，信任是通过输入和输出建立的，而不是通过盒子内的内容建立的。一个例子是向他们提供一个数字身份证图像，该图像与只有政府持有私钥的加密签名配对。他们没有计算上可行的方法来编辑图像并仍然使其与配对签名匹配。它可以根据政府的公钥轻松验证，并且如果没有政府的私钥，他们无法重新签名。它不依赖于硬件限制。

事实上，对硬件的如此多的关注意味着这里可能有更深层次的动机，例如监视被装扮成方便。

> When two people interact there’s a trust/risk calculation on both sides.

You should never base your trust on the other party having a piece of hardware that has restrictions that you approve of. That is fragile, especially in a world where some people are better at making or modifying hardware than others. It is also a fundamental violation of basic freedoms to prevent people from modifying hardware that they own, and not something you can reliably police, and thus is a terrible way to establish trust from a technical perspective.

It's much better to base trust on established cryptographic methods on a protocol level. You treat them as a black box, and the trust is established by the inputs and outputs, not what's inside the box. An example of this would be handing them an image of a digital ID paired with a cryptographic signature that only the government holds the private keys to. They have no computationally viable way to edit the image and still have it match the paired signature. It's easily verified based on the government's public key, and they cannot re-sign it without the government's private key. It doesn't depend on hardware restrictions.

The fact that there is so much focus on hardware means there are likely deeper motives here, e.g. surveillence being dressed up as convenience.

楼层
pimterry 2026/07/01 02:07
这始终是一个反竞争问题。

如果我们限制对特定实现证明的服务访问，那么新实现的出现就会变得更加困难。谁控制这个过程并不重要。

从这个意义上说，它总是不好的。例如，在这种特定情况下，它直接阻止替代 Android ROM 和 Android 主要兼容设备（如各种 Linux 手机）的出现。

有时，这种缺点可能是值得的，但它始终是一个缺点，为了技术生态系统及其周围商业市场的健康，我们应该在此基础上尽可能强烈地阻止证明。

It's an anti-competitive concern all the time.

If we gatekeep service access to specific implementation attestations, it becomes much harder for new implementations to emerge. It doesn't really matter who controls the process.

In that sense, it's always bad. In this specific scenario for example it directly blocks emergence of alternative Android ROMs and Android-mostly-compatible devices like the various Linux phones.

There may be times where that downside is worthwhile, but it's always a downside, and we should very strongly discourage attestation wherever possible on that basis for the health of both the tech ecosystem and the business market around it.

garaetjjte 2026/07/01 02:34
唯一可接受的证明使用是代表设备所有者完成的。因此，如果他们让我提交我的帐户允许的密钥列表，那很好，但任何其他用途都是对我可以在自己的设备上运行的软件的邪恶限制。
Only acceptable use of attestation is when done on behalf of device owner. So if they let me submit list of keys allowed for my account, fine, but any other use is just evil restriction of what software I can run on my own devices.

Retr0id 2026/06/30 23:21
证明的一个假设有用用途是，承诺安全处理个人数据的公司实际上可以通过开源其服务器端代码并​​使用可重复构建与远程证明相结合的方式向最终用户证明这一点，以向客户端证明服务器端在安全区域内未经修改地运行。

不过，我很难想出它在最终用户客户端的有用用途。

A hypothetical useful use of attestation is that a company promising to process personal data securely could actually prove it to end-users, by open-sourcing their server-side code and using reproducible builds combined with remote attestation, to prove to the client that the server-side is running unmodified within a secure enclave.

I struggle to think of a useful use for it on the end-user client side, though.

Isn’t the client-side case something like “the banking app you’re entering your account password into is the binary the bank created and not a compromised binary that will drain your bank account”?
楼层
Wicher 2026/07/01 03:12
Android 上已经存在这种情况。

如果您已经安装了 RealActualBankApp（ID 为 real.actual.bank.app）一次（无论来源如何！），那么就不可能再安装具有相同 id 但使用不同公钥签名的另一个应用程序（故事的过于简化的版本，存在密钥滚动方案）。

但是，您可以安装一个冒名顶替者应用程序 还 名为 RealActualBankApp，具有相同的图标。它需要使用不同的 ID。

因此，我们遇到了与银行网站 URL 相同的身份混淆问题或伪问题。 ID/URL 显示在哪里，用户是否知道它应该是 mybank.com 而不是 mybank-incorporated.com ？

This is kind of already the case on Android.

If you've installed RealActualBankApp (with the ID of real.actual.bank.app) once (from whatever source!) then there cannot be another app installed with that same id but signed with a different public key (oversimplified version of the story, there is a key rollover scheme).

You can however install an imposter app that's also called RealActualBankApp, with the same icon. It'll need to go by a different ID.

So then we're down to the same problem, or pseudo-problem, of identity confusion, as we have for banking website URLs. Where is the ID/URL shown, and does the user know that it should be mybank.com and not mybank-incorporated.com ?

楼层
Retr0id 2026/07/01 03:21
Android 密钥证明绑定到创建密钥的应用程序，因此如果它们在身份验证期间使用证明，这确实会阻止全功能克隆工作。但它并不能阻止仅用于网络钓鱼凭据的虚假应用程序。
Android Key Attestations are bound to the app that minted the key, so this does prevent a fully-functional clone from working if they use attestation during auth. But it doesn't prevent a fake app that only exists to phish credentials.
楼层
summm 2026/07/01 02:02
不，这只需要软件的可公开验证的签名，并且用户只需选择让他们的操作系统验证它。无需远程认证或其他交接控制权。
No, this would just require a publicly verifyable signature of the software, and the user would just choose to have their operating system verify it. No remote attestation or other hand-over-your-controls necessary.
楼层
[deleted] 2026/07/01 01:24 deleted

[已删除]

[deleted]

楼层
microtonal 2026/07/01 01:25
绝对不是一直都不好。例如，GrapheneOS 提供了 Auditor 应用程序，您可以使用该应用程序从另一部手机或服务器验证操作系统是否未被篡改。它还使用远程认证。

所以，肯定有有用的应用程序。

Definitely not bad all the time. For instance, GrapheneOS provides the Auditor app, with which you can verify from another phone or from a server that the OS is not tampered with. It also uses remote attestation.

So, there are certainly useful applications.

楼层
Retr0id 2026/07/01 01:46
我质疑 Auditor 的用处。例如，它可以标记 GrapheneOS 的修改版本是否已启动。但是刷新 GrapheneOS 的修改版本需要擦除用户数据，当所有数据不存在时您会注意到这一点。除非有人使用漏洞，但密钥证明无法检测漏洞。

我想如果你购买了已经安装了 GrapheneOS 的设备，你可以用它来验证安装。但这也可以通过自己重新刷新已知的良好图像来实现。

I question the usefulness of Auditor. It can flag if a modified version of GrapheneOS has been booted, for example. But flashing a modified version of GrapheneOS requires erasing userdata, which you'd notice the moment all your data isn't there. Unless someone uses an exploit, but Key Attestation cannot detect exploits.

I suppose if you've bought a device with GrapheneOS already installed, you can use it to verify the installation. But that could also be achieved by reflashing a known-good image yourself.

楼层
microtonal 2026/07/01 02:20
基本同意。虽然我认为有一些有用的应用：1.你提到的那个； 2. 防止安装恶意图像（例如，因为您的浏览器/证书存储遭到破坏）； 3. 一种复杂的攻击，攻击者在某个时刻知道您的凭据（例如 PIN），在手机无人看管时提取您的数据，闪烁受损图像并恢复数据（目的是监视您的手机）。

诚然，其中大多数可能是国家级攻击，但我认为一些 GrapheneOS 用户是此类攻击的目标。此外，在全新安装后运行 Auditor 也没有什么坏处，可以防止出现第二种情况。只需一分钟，安全总比后悔好。

Largely agreed. Though I think there are useful applications: 1. the one you mention; 2. to protect against installation of a malicious image (e.g. because your browser/certificate store compromised); 3. a sophisticated attack where an attacker knows your credentials at some point (e.g. PIN), extract your data when the phone is unattended, flashes a compromised image, and restores the data (with the goal to surveil your phone).

Admittedly, most of these are probably nation state-level attacks, but I think some GrapheneOS users are the target of such attacks. Also, it doesn't hurt to run Auditor after a fresh install to protect against the second scenario. It only takes a minute, better safe than sorry.

楼层
hmlwilliams 2026/06/30 21:41
正如这里所概述的： https://grapheneos.org/articles/attestation-compatibility-gu...，GrapheneOS 并没有实现一些独特的东西，它正在实现 Android 硬件认证： https://developer.android.com/privacy-and-security/security-...
As outlined here: https://grapheneos.org/articles/attestation-compatibility-gu..., GrapheneOS isn't implementing something unique, it's implementing Android Hardware Attestation: https://developer.android.com/privacy-and-security/security-...
楼层
Retr0id 2026/06/30 21:43
Android 密钥证明生成使用植根于硬件供应商 CA 的证书链进行签名的证明。例如，如果您在 Pixel 设备上的 GrapheneOS 上使用密钥证明，它会证明您正在使用 GrapheneOS 的 AVB 密钥，但该证明仍由 Google 证书链签名。

“添加对 GrapheneOS 的支持”意味着将其 AVB 密钥列入白名单，一般来说，它不会为第三方实现打开大门。

如果您在选择的其他设备上运行 GrapheneOS，证明将会失败。

如果您运行您选择的非 GrapheneOS 自定义 ROM，证明将会失败。

Android Key Attestation produces attestations that are signed with a certificate chain rooted in the hardware vendor's CA. If you use Key Attestation on GrapheneOS on a Pixel device for example, it attests that you're using GrapheneOS's AVB keys, but that attestation is still signed by a Google certificate chain.

"Adding support for GrapheneOS" means allowlisting their AVB keys specifically, it does not open a door for 3rd party implementations in general.

If you run GrapheneOS on a different device of your choosing, attestation would fail.

If you run a non-GrapheneOS custom ROM of your choosing, attestation would fail.

楼层
NotPractical 2026/07/01 00:31
更不用说 GrapheneOS 的自签名自定义构建了。
Not to mention self-signed custom builds of GrapheneOS.
楼层
altairprime 2026/07/01 08:07
建议政府放弃在线验证政府颁发的身份证件的斗争，而不是提出不基于证明的替代解决方案，这似乎是错失了改变路线的机会。通过什么方法进行防御转售 应该 政府要验证他们在网上发布和控制的身份吗？
Proposing that governments give up the fight for verifying government-issued identity papers online rather than proposing an alternate solution that’s not attestation-based seems like a missed opportunity to change their course. By what defensible-against-resale method should governments be authenticating the identities they issue and control online?

testhest 2026/06/30 21:22

Agreed, it should be open standards only.

Retr0id 2026/06/30 21:32

No! An open standard for remote attestation would still be remote attestation.
楼层
u1hcw9nx 2026/06/30 21:50
这仅反映了他们目前的市场份额。欧盟从法律上禁止成员国强制使用智能手机来获取公共服务。欧盟明确预见到完全依赖 iOS 和 Android 的危险，并设计了 EUDI 钱包框架以允许其他物理形式因素。例如;

1. 智能卡（例如当前国民身份证）

2. 独立硬件令牌和 USB 密钥

This is only reflects their market share for now. The EU legally forbids member states from making a smartphone mandatory to access public services. The EU explicitly anticipated the danger of relying entirely on the iOS and Android and designed the EUDI Wallet framework to allow for other physical form factors. For example;

1. Smart Cards (for example The Current National ID)

2. Standalone Hardware Tokens & USB Keys

楼层
deaux 2026/07/01 02:12
> 欧盟从法律上禁止成员国强制使用智能手机来获取公共服务。

是的，我确信他们仍然允许邮寄晦涩的表格来获取公共服务，然后需要 3 周的时间来处理。

如果欧盟真的想“预见”这种危险，他们早就做到了 强制的 在 EUDI 钱包中包含物理形状因素。事实上，他们并不介意这种危险，所以它是可选的，你可以打赌大多数国家不会包括这样的危险，让谷歌和苹果成为唯一的选择。

> The EU legally forbids member states from making a smartphone mandatory to access public services.

Yes, I'm sure they'll still allow for mail-in of obscure forms to access public services, which will then take 3 weeks to be processed.

If the EU actually wanted to "anticipate" this danger they'd have made it mandatory to include a physical form factor in EUDI wallets. In reality, they don't mind this danger, so it's optional, and you can bet most countries won't include one and make Google and Apple the only options.

Zak 2026/06/30 22:47
人们不断地根据以下方面来构建此类辩论： 修修补补。

这是关于所有权，而不是修补。这是为了防止大型企业对政府服务如何运作以及人们如何与政府互动拥有最终决定权。

People keep framing these sorts of debates in terms of tinkering.

It's about ownership, not tinkering. It's about preventing megacorporations from having the last word about how government services can function and how people can interact with them.

楼层
Luker88 2026/06/30 20:19
“功能性文盲”意味着当您 能 阅读您的母语，您将无法正确理解您刚刚阅读的内容。

各州的税率似乎有所不同，从低至 8%（丹麦）到 43%（罗马尼亚）。

这也不是一个明确定义的目标，因为最好根据 X 年平均学校的阅读理解能力或类似的水平来确定费率。

"functionally illiterate" means that while you can read your native language, you will not correctly understand what you have just read.

Rates seem to vary state by state, from as low as 8% (denmark) to 43% (romania).

It's also not a clearly defined target, since it would be better to have rates based on the reading comprehension of the average school at year X or something similar.

楼层
ralferoo 2026/06/30 20:50
我对这个定义很好奇，因为这不是我以前考虑过的事情，而且谷歌搜索似乎让水变得更加浑浊。

如果您可以大声朗读该语言但无法理解它，如果您也无法理解对您所说的相同内容，那么这是“功能性文盲”吗？看来这是关于理解能力，而不是识字能力。

虽然我刚刚想到的一件事是，如果你的阅读水平很低，你可能会用你所有的认知来阅读，这样你就没有多余的能力去理解——这对我来说经常是这样，例如在中文中，我可以读出整篇文章，然后老师问这篇文章是关于什么的，我只是想“我不知道，我没有考虑这个，但我想我理解了一切”。

这绝对是一个不同的问题，能够读出这些单词，但只是不知道这些单词的含义，无论你是读到还是听到它们。

它必须是您的母语还是任何语言？并不是想吹毛求疵，只是感觉这个短语也可以有效地应用于外语。

I'm curious about this definition, just because it's not something I've ever considered before and googling seems to muddy the water even more.

Is it "functionally illiterate" if you can read the language aloud and not understand it, if you also wouldn't have understood the same thing spoken to you? That seems like it's about comprehension ability, not literacy.

Although one thing that just occurred to me is that if your reading level is low, you might be using all your cognition on reading so that you don't have spare capacity to understand as well - that's frequently the case for me with e.g. Chinese where I can read an entire passage out and then the teacher asks what the passage was about and I'm just thinking "I dunno, I wasn't thinking about that but I think I understood everything".

And that's definitely a different problem to being able to sound out the words, but just having no idea what those words mean, whether you read them or heard them.

And does it have to be your native language, or in any language? Not trying to nitpick, it just feels like the phrase can be usefully applied to a foreign language too.

楼层
gcr 2026/06/30 21:34
上游的海报谈论的是理解和价值体系，而不是识字能力。

例如，“功能性文盲”是人们在描述政治观点相反或社会经济地位较低的人时所使用的画笔。

posters upthread are talking about comprehension and value systems, not literacy.

"functionally illiterate" is the brush that one paints with when describing people of opposing political viewpoint or lower socioeconomic status, for example.

https://www.southtyneside.gov.uk/article/16247/Public-Health...

> 指南告诉我们，东北地区的平均阅读年龄低于全国平均水平，为 9 至 11 岁。考虑到这一点，《卫报》的阅读年龄为 14 岁，《太阳报》的阅读年龄为 8 岁。

健康素养尤其是医疗保健领域的一个主要问题

https://literacytrust.org.uk/parents-and-families/adult-lite...

> 四分之一的苏格兰成年人（26.7% / 931,000 人）由于缺乏识字技能而面临挑战。

我觉得这个页面有点讽刺，因为他们声称 18% 是六分之一，但 17.4% 是五分之一。看来算数也是一个巨大的挑战。

根据维基百科，美国也好不到哪儿去

> 2023 年，28% 的成年人得分为 1 级或以下，29% 为 2 级，44% 为 3 级或以上

> 分数低于 1 级的成人可以理解结构最少的简单句子和短段落，但难以理解多步骤指令或复杂句子

> 得分达到 3 级或以上的成人被视为“熟练处理文本中的信息和想法”

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Literacy_in_the_United_States

Dunno what the OP meant, but in the UK

https://www.southtyneside.gov.uk/article/16247/Public-Health...

> Guidance tells us the average reading age in the North East is lower than the national average at between 9 to 11 years. To put that into context The Guardian Newspaper has a reading age of 14 and the Sun Newspaper has a reading age of 8.

Health literacy specifically is a major problem in healthcare

https://literacytrust.org.uk/parents-and-families/adult-lite...

> 1 in 4 (26.7% / 931,000 people) adults in Scotland experience challenges due to their lack of literacy skills.

I find that page somewhat ironic as they claim 18% is one in six, but 17.4% is one in five. Seems numeracy is as big a challenge.

The US is no better according to wikipedia

> In 2023, 28% of adults scored at or below Level 1, 29% at Level 2, and 44% at Level 3 or above

> Adults scoring below Level 1 can comprehend simple sentences and short paragraphs with minimal structure but will struggle with multi-step instructions or complex sentences

> Adults scoring at Level 3 or above are considered "proficient at working with information and ideas in texts

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Literacy_in_the_United_States

可以肯定的是，在大多数国家，平均每个人每年的阅读量少于一本书，因此一半的人口阅读量低于这个数字。我认识一些人，他们从高中开始就没有读过书，当时他们是被迫读的。

microtonal 2026/06/30 22:11
首先，GrapheneOS支持远程证明。因此，如果他们想要安全，他们就可以得到。其次，欧盟目前对主权的关注是一个机会之窗，与两年前相比，现在有更好的机会来应对这一问题。
First, GrapheneOS supports remote attestation. So if they want their security, they can have it. Second, the current focus of the EU on sovereignty is a window of opportunity and there are better opportunities to fight this than two years ago.

ivolimmen 2026/06/30 19:52

楼层
seba_dos1 2026/06/30 21:49
GrapheneOS 也支持证明，所以即使他们成功了，它也可能会变成给谷歌、苹果和 GrapheneOS 的一份礼物。它是需要反对的硬件认证，因为它本质上是用户敌对的，允许单一流行的 Android 发行版在宏伟的计划中没有多大作用。
GrapheneOS supports attestation too, so even if they succeed it will likely just turn into a gift to Google, Apple and GrapheneOS. It's hardware attestation that needs to be opposed as it's inherently user hostile, allowing a single popular Android distro doesn't do much in the grand scheme of things.
楼层
microtonal 2026/06/30 22:14
每个Android系统都支持远程认证。它是 AOSP 的一部分。谷歌只是决定不使用它，因为 Play Integrity 允许他们锁定手机制造商并迫使他们（根据泄露的协议）预安装一堆谷歌应用程序，并要求运行 Play 服务和系统上的一些其他特权组件。
Every Android system support remote attestation. It's part of AOSP. Google just decided not to use it, because Play Integrity allows them to lock in phone manufacturers and force them (per leaked agreements) to preinstall a bunch of Google apps and require to run Play Services and some other components privileged on the system.
楼层
ValleZ 2026/07/01 00:14
Play 完整性检查应用程序是否被篡改。硬件证明只能保证密钥的来源，不能用于检查应用程序的完整性。
Play Integrity checks if app was tampered with. Hardware attestations can only guarantee key's source and cannot be used to check app integrity.
楼层
microtonal 2026/07/01 01:14
我特别提到了 Play Integrity 中的远程证明功能，并且该功能可以由 AOSP API 替换，因为链接的帖子是关于远程证明的。

Play Integrity 实际上同时做到了这两点，并且通过远程认证对于在强级别上通过 Play Integrity 是必要的。此级别使用远程认证，因为修改后的操作系统可能会欺骗 DroidGuard。

如果我的评论不清楚我所指的内容，我很抱歉。

I specifically referred to the remote attestation functionality in Play Integrity and that that can be replaced by AOSPs APIs, since the linked post is about remote attestation.

Play Integrity actually does both and passing remote attestation is necessary to pass Play Integrity at the strong level. Remote attestation is used for this level, since a modified OS could fool DroidGuard.

I'm sorry if my comment was not clear in what I was referring to.

楼层
seba_dos1 2026/06/30 22:40
AOSP 中的某些内容并不意味着您的发行版必须保留它。此外，Android系统的世界并没有终结。
Something being in AOSP doesn't mean your distro has to retain it. Besides, the world doesn't end on Android systems.
楼层
chadgpt3 2026/07/01 00:40
越多越好——被迫维护 Google 竞争对手的最新列表（包括一些不保证证明密钥安全的列表，因此坏人会假装是这些竞争对手，无论如何你都将被迫允许）可能会让一些人重新考虑该功能是否真正带来任何价值。
The more the better - being forced to maintain an up-to-date list of Google competitors (including some that don't keep attestation keys secure, so the bad guys will pretend to be those and you'll be forced to allow it anyway) may make some reconsider whether the feature actually brings any value.

Android 和 Apple 设备有何特别之处？

楼层
seba_dos1 2026/06/30 23:47
Android和Apple设备让远程服务器验证本地应用程序及其运行的系统是否未被用户修改，如果被修改则拒绝提供服务。这就是它们的特别之处，很难想象 Debian 的通用安装如何在不严格限制用户的情况下做到这一点。

这是一种定义不明确的“安全”措施，无论出现在哪里都应该遭到恶意反对。

Android and Apple devices let the remote server verify whether the local application and the system it runs on haven't been modified by the user and refuse providing services if they were. That's what makes them special, it's hard to imagine how a, say, generic installation of Debian could do that without severely restricting the user.

It's an ill-defined "security" measure that should be viciously opposed anywhere it shows up.

artk42 2026/07/01 00:54

楼层
microtonal 2026/06/30 22:05
此外，正如文章所述，Play Integrity 很可能违反 DMA。如果您居住在欧盟并受​​此影响（或者如果您计划改用替代方案，则将来会受此影响），请向 EU DMA 团队发送消息：

https://digital-markets-act.ec.europa.eu/contact-us-eu-citiz...

他们获得的实际公民受此打击的例子越多越好。最近，当谷歌推出基于设备的新验证码以及大众汽车开始阻止 GrapheneOS 时，我都发送了消息。当然，不要大喊大叫，耐心并充分论证地解释为什么您会受到“游戏诚信”的影响，以及您认为“游戏诚信”如何被用来执行双头垄断+违背欧盟主权。

此外，对于使用 Play Integrity 的应用程序，请向该公司发送电子邮件。对他们的样板回复做出后续反应（这似乎在大众汽车方面慢慢取得了一些进展）。还要对他们的应用程序留下一星评论，并在评论中解释他们破坏了对您系统的支持。

我知道这一切看起来都毫无希望。但尤其是 GrapheneOS 现在势头强劲，迅速获得了更多用户。感觉现在是我们可以认真影响事情变得更好的时刻。现在有大约 50 万用户。如果每个人都积极参与，我们就能取得进展。

Also, as the article says, Play Integrity is most likely a violation of the DMA. Send a message to the EU DMA Team if you live in the EU and are affected by this (or affected by this in the future, if you plan to switch to an alternative):

https://digital-markets-act.ec.europa.eu/contact-us-eu-citiz...

The more examples they get of actual citizens that get hit by this, the better. I have recently sent messages when Google introduced their new device-based recaptcha and when Volkswagen started blocking GrapheneOS. Of course, do not yell, explain patiently and with good argumentation why you are affected by Play Integrity and how you believe Play Integrity is used to enforce the duopoly + goes counter EU sovereignty.

Also, for apps that use Play Integrity, e-mail the company. React to their boilerplate replies with follow-ups (this slowly seems to get some headway with VW). Also leave a one-star review on their app, explaining in the review that they broke support for your system.

I know that this can all seem hopeless. But especially GrapheneOS is getting a lot of momentum now, rapidly gaining more users. It feels like it is a moment in time where we can seriously influence things for the better. There are ~500,000s users now. If everyone actively participates, we can move the needle.

楼层
whizzter 2026/06/30 19:41
老实说，只要架构存在致命缺陷（即使方便），它就只是解决更大问题的创可贴。

这些移动ID太强大了，签署合同、转移所有资金或接受贷款，监管也通过要求高风险贷方等进行额外检查来掩盖这一点。

恕我直言，德国正朝着正确的方向前进，他们通过 NFC 启用了他们的身份证（瑞典有关于它们的信息，但没有启用程序），然后与应用程序配对，因此该卡充当第二个因素，使应用程序本身不那么安全问题，因为用户将需要物理启用它（遗憾的是 NFC 配对有点繁琐..但我将其作为所有重要传输的安全选项）。

Honestly, as long as the architectures is fatally flawed (Even if convenient) it's just bandaids over a larger issue.

These mobile id's are too powerful, signing contracts, transfering all your funds or taking loans, regulation is also papering it over a bit by requiring high-stakes lenders,etc to do additional checks.

Germany was going in the right direction imho, they NFC enabled their ID cards (Sweden has info on them but no enablement procedures) that is then paired with the app, so the card acts as a 2nd factor that makes the app itself less of a security issue since a user will be required to physically enable it (sadly the NFC pairings are kinda fiddly.. but I'd take that as a security option for all non-trivial transfers).

楼层
doikor 2026/06/30 19:50
> 这些移动ID太强大了，签署合同、转移所有资金或接受贷款，监管也通过要求高风险贷方等进行额外检查来掩盖这一点。

欧盟的许多国家已经通过一些国家等效系统完成了所有这些工作（例如在芬兰，主要是通过银行凭证）。

事实上，当有足够的资金流动时，就会进行额外的检查。例如，当我签署一套公寓的银行贷款时，我必须在 24 小时后再次签署，才能真正确定我想要签署。

对于较小（但仍然足够大）的东西，通常会在使用银行凭据实际正确登录后以短信验证的形式启动第二个“第二因素”（银行凭据本身也具有适当的双因素身份验证）

> These mobile id's are too powerful, signing contracts, transfering all your funds or taking loans, regulation is also papering it over a bit by requiring high-stakes lenders,etc to do additional checks.

Many countries in the EU already have all of that just done though some national equilevant system (for example here in Finland mainly with bank credentials).

And in fact additonal checks are done when enough money is moving. For example when I signed my bank loan for an apartment I had to sign it again after 24 hours just to be really really sure that I wanted to sign it.

For smaller (but still big enough) stuff a second "second factor" usually kicks in usually in the form of a sms verification after the actual proper login with bank credentials (which has a proper 2 factor auth in itself too)

楼层
donjoe 2026/06/30 22:08
芬兰有银行绑定系统真是太好了。 我希望他们的实施不会像例如那样糟糕。瑞典银行 ID。

从理论上讲，BankID 是一项很好的技术。然而，它只发放给在瑞典税务机关注册并持有瑞典银行账户的人。

现在所有日常活动都与 BankID 绑定：需要预约医生吗？ -> 需要BankID；想在 Blocket 上买东西吗？ -> 需要银行 ID。

作为一个经常在瑞典待一段时间但没有瑞典税的欧洲人，我感觉自己被排除在这个国家的线上和部分线下活动之外。

It's great you do have a bank-bound system in Finland. I hope their implementation is not as bad as e.g. the Swedish BankID.

BankID is _in theory_ a nice technology. However, it is only handed out to people registered with the Swedish tax authorities holding a Swedish bank account.

All daily activities are nowadays bound to BankID: need a doctor's appointment? -> needs BankID; Want to buy something on Blocket? -> needs BankID.

As an European frequently spending some time in Sweden not in possession of a Swedish tax #, I feel very much excluded from online and partially offline activities in this country.

楼层
whizzter 2026/06/30 23:42
好吧，这就是整个数字钱包的要点，将会有一个过渡期，因为这里的所有内容或多或少都被硬编码为bankid，更不用说大量带有瑞典身份语义假设的代码（这确实与其他国家不同）。

但从好的方面来看，瑞典国家 eID 解决方案计划在今年年底前交付，希望大多数组织将开始迁移或至少双重支持它们，并在此过程中修复其服务以支持外国 eID。

Well that is the point of this entire digital wallet thingy, there's going to be a transition period since everything here is more or less hardcoded to bankid not to mention tons of code with presumptions about Swedish identity semantics (that do differ from other countries).

But on the plus-side the Swedish state-eID solutions is planned to be delivered by end of year and hopefully most organizations will start migrating or at least dual-supporting them and in doing so also fix their services to support foreign eID's in the process.

楼层
estebank 2026/06/30 22:33
我看到很多国家都在全力解决这个问题。很少需要单一实体身份证和无处不在的现金支付的时代似乎即将结束。对于经常旅行的人、首先在某个地方定居的移民或国外公民来说，这使得事情变得比以前更加困难。
This is a problem I'm seeing a lot of countries rushing full steam ahead. The age of a single physical ID that's only rarely needed and ubiquitous cash payments seems to be coming to an end. For anyone who travels a lot, migrants first settling in a place, or citizens abroad, this makes things even harder than they already were.
楼层
StanislavPetrov 2026/07/01 01:33
如果您违反了政府的法令，政府有能力将您排除在参与社会（或生存）所需的日常活动之外，这是数字身份/数字货币的一个功能，而不是一个错误。
The ability of the government(s) to exclude you from every day activities required for participation in society (or survival) if you run afoul of their edicts is a feature of digital ID/digital currency, not a bug.
楼层
whizzter 2026/06/30 23:49
再说一遍，这一切仍然与手机这一单一设备相关，如果它被黑客入侵，游戏就真的结束了，而且 Android 或 iOS 生态系统中存在足够大的漏洞，可能会引起蠕虫攻击，很多人可能会被集体利用。

当然，24 小时延迟或短信代码是两种方式，但它们完全属于创可贴类别。

过去，我们曾经断开银行加密狗的连接，银行发出一次性挑战，您输入响应以及您的用户名。现在这些也有缺点，但至少它是完全气隙的。

Again, it's all still tied to that one device, the phone, if it's hacked it's really game over and with a big enough hole in the Android or iOS ecosystem that could be wormable a lot of people could be exploited en-masse.

Sure a 24h delay or SMS code are 2 way but they fully fall into the bandaid category.

In the past we used to have disconnected dongles for banking, the bank issued a one-time challange and you entered the response along with your username. Now there are disadvantages with those also but at least it was fully airgapped.

楼层
ulrikrasmussen 2026/06/30 19:44
在我看来，即使依赖 Android 的硬件认证 API 而不是 Play Integrity，也是对数字自治的攻击。任何依赖于用户远程认证的安全功能 整个平台 是政府越权，因为它最终赋予政府选择可接受的操作系统的权力。这种权力迟早会被滥用，向操作系统开发商施加压力，要求他们为情报机构安装后门。不，要求人们拥有两部智能手机并不能解决这个问题。

基于合适的 ZKP 方案和/或盲签名的匿名数字年龄验证不需要通用操作系统，它只需要一些加密原语和一组设备绑定密钥。要求欧盟开发具有这些确切功能的专用硬件代币，并将其免费提供给所有公民作为应用程序的替代品并不过分。这也使欧盟公民可以自由选择不拥有智能手机，而不会受到严格限制对数字服务的访问。

Even relying on Android's hardware attestation API instead of Play Integrity is an attack on digital autonomy in my opinion. Any security feature which relies on remote attestation of the users entire platform is government overreach as it ultimately gives the government the power to choose what operating systems are acceptable. It is only a matter of time before this power will be misused to put pressure on OS developers to install backdoors for the intelligence agencies. And no, asking people to own two smartphones is not a solution to this problem.

Anonymous digital age verification based on a suitable ZKP scheme and/or blind signatures does not require a general purpose operating system, it just requires a few cryptographic primitives and a set of device-bound keys. It is not too much to ask that the EU develops a specialized hardware token with these exact capabilities and offer them for free to all citizens as an alternative to the app. This also gives the citizens of EU the freedom to choose not to own a smartphone without having their access to digital services severely restricted.

楼层
whazor 2026/06/30 20:50
我同意加强硬件安全。对于银行和政府来说都是如此。

但它绝不能限制在手机上运行定制软件的能力。尤其是不强制每个人都获得谷歌/苹果签名的手机。

就像我在手机上安装 GrapheneOS 一样。银行/政府应用程序应该可以工作。但我相信通过加强硬件安全也可以实现这一点。

I'm ok with enforcing hardware security. Both for banks and governments.

But it must not limit the ability of running custom software on a phone. And especially not enforcing every person to get a Google/Apple signed phone.

Like if I get GrapheneOS on my phone. Banking/gov apps should work. But I believe this could be possible with enforcing hardware security as well.

楼层
TingPing 2026/06/30 21:25
信任链总是有一个软件层。我不相信你想要的事情是可能的。

我觉得银行的谈话点很奇怪，为什么他们很特别，他们是否更有针对性。感觉就像是一个妖怪“想想你的钱！”

The chain of trust always has a software layer. I don’t believe what you want is possible.

I find the bank talking point strange, why are they special, are they even targeted more. It just feels like a boogeyman “think of your money!”

楼层
microtonal 2026/06/30 22:24
出于所有实际目的，可以这样做。引导 ROM 仅引导供应商签名的引导加载程序，引导加载程序验证操作系统内核等，直到您拥有经过完全验证的引导链。与主 CPU 完全分离的安全飞地，操作系统使用其防篡改存储中的私钥执行证明，并嵌入引导加载程序的验证结果。可能存在一些漏洞，但除引导 ROM 外，大多数漏洞都可以通过更新修复。

Android系统偶尔会被破坏的原因是大多数Android手机的安全性很差，并且不使用安全的飞地/处理器等（iPhone从5s + Google/三星以来通过Titan M/Knox Vault已经使用了很多年）。相反，他们使用 TrustZone，它在与主操作系统相同的 CPU/RAM 上设置 TEE。当然，它使用内存保护进行分离，但往往容易受到旁道攻击。这也是很多Android手机会被Cellebrite秒破解的原因（最近就公开了这样一个Mediatek TEE漏洞[1]）。

[1] https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/03/this-android-...

For all practical purposes it's possible to do this. The boot ROM only boots a vendor-signed bootloader, the bootloader verifies the OS kernel, etc., until you have a fully verified boot chain. A secure enclave, which is completely separated from the main CPU and OS performs the attestation using a private key in its tamper-resistant storage and embeds the results of verification by the bootloader. There may be some vulnerabilities, but most of them can be fixed in updates, with exception of the boot ROM.

The reason why the system gets broken in Android occasionally is that most Android phones have terrible security and do not use a secure enclave/processor, etc. (which the iPhone had since 5s + Google/Samsung for quite some years through Titan M/Knox Vault). Instead they use TrustZone, which set up a TEE on the same CPU/RAM as the main OS. Of course, it uses memory protection for separation, but is often vulnerable to side-channel attacks. This is also the reason many Android phones will be cracked by Cellebrite in seconds (recently such a Mediatek TEE vulnerability was made public [1]).

[1] https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2026/03/this-android-...

楼层
summm 2026/07/01 01:51
没有。仍然不可能让其他人（政府或银行）控制您的手机，同时运行您独自以更高权限控制的软件。 请不要将其与“由于草率的代码而实际上很难实现”混为一谈。 此外，您的攻击者模型仍然是“偶尔邪恶的政府机构或邪恶的私人公司想要破解并阅读您的消息”，而这里讨论的是更基本的“邪恶的政府或滥用职权的公司首先控制您的手机，并且只能远程控制它，您无法使用真正安全的应用程序”
Nope. It is still not possible to give someone else (the government, or the bank) control over your phone while at the same time run software that you alone control with higher privileges. Please don't mix that up with "is practically hard to implement because of sloppy code. Also your attacker model is still "occasional evil government agency or evil private corporation wants to crack and read your messages", while what is discussed here is more fundamental "evil government or abusive corporation controls your phone in the first place, and can just remote control it you can't use really secure apps"
楼层
whazor 2026/07/01 19:57
我希望政府和银行允许开源软件，而不是控制我的手机。

例如，我基本上信任从 GrapheneOS 网站下载的 ROM。我希望政府、银行或一些独立的开放基金会也能够批准该 ROM，这样认证就可以与它一起使用。

更像是 CA 证书的工作方式：并不完美，但也不锁定于某个供应商。

I want governments and banks to allow open-source software, not control my phone.

For example, I essentially trust the ROM I download from the GrapheneOS website. What I want is for governments, banks, or some independent open foundation to be able to approve that ROM too, so attestation can work with it.

More like how CA certificates work: not perfect, but not locked to one vendor either.

楼层
summm 2026/07/02 02:18
现在您可以在两个经过批准的 ROM 之间进行选择。进步不是很大吗？一旦 GrapheneOS 实施了政府不喜欢的对用户有益的东西，批准就会被收回。这也是为什么 GrapheneOS 可能根本不会考虑这样做。 所以你想要一些谷歌和石墨烯都没有提供的操作系统功能，你又不走运了。 CA 是完全不同的东西，并且在可以集中控制的方面受到更多限制。每个人都可以去 CA，获取其域的证书，并将其与任何服务器软件一起使用，甚至可以与他们自己编译的软件一起使用。
So now you have the choice between two approved ROMs. Not a lot of improvement? And as soon as GrapheneOS implements something beneficial to the users that the government does not like, the approval will be taken back. That's also why GrapheneOS will probably not even think about doing that. So you want some OS functionality neither Google nor Graphene offer, you're, again, out of luck. CA is something completely different, and much more limited in what can be centrally controlled. Everybody can go to a CA, get a certificate for their domain, and use it with any server software, even with software they compiled themselves.
楼层
ulrikrasmussen 2026/06/30 21:47
不过，年龄验证中的软件层并不需要信任。可能发生的最糟糕的情况是，受损的软件层窃取了您的年龄凭证，但它是匿名设计的，因此您不会冒金钱或帐户被盗或其他任何东西的风险。这使得它成为与银行案例不同的威胁模型。
The software layer in age verification is not necessary to trust though. The worst that could happen is that a compromised software layer steals your age credential, but it is by design anonymous so you don't risk getting your money or account stolen or anything. This makes it a different threat model from the banking case.

fsflover 2026/07/01 07:10

Have a look at Heads. It uses TPM with a hardware key to verify the boot integrity without proprietary blobs and with full control of the user. Works for me.
楼层
Retr0id 2026/06/30 21:37
您可以将密钥材料存储在硬件支持的飞地中，而无需涉及远程证明。如果有人修改了设备/客户端并将密钥存储在其他地方，那是他们的责任 - 他们只会削弱自己的安全性。
You can store key material in hardware-backed enclaves without involving remote attestation. If someone has a modified device/client that stores the keys elsewhere, that's on them - they're only weakening their own security.
楼层
Meneth 2026/06/30 21:47
你不能两者兼得。 “硬件安全”意味着制造商决定哪个操作系统可以运行并且您无法覆盖它。
You can't have both. "Hardware security" means the manufacturer decides which OS can run and you can't override it.
楼层
whazor 2026/07/01 19:51
实际上，我今天可以在我的 Framework 笔记本电脑上设置真正的硬件强制启动完整性，无论是在 Arch 上使用 sbctl，还是在 NixOS 上使用 Lanzaboote 或 Limine。

硬件安全还有很多未解决的问题，所以这个今天肯定没有解决。但我不明白为什么这是不可能的。欧盟可以推动该行业转向尊重用户所有权的认证模型，而不是将所有内容锁定在供应商控制的软件后面。

Practically speaking, I can set up real hardware-enforced boot integrity on my Framework laptop today, both on Arch with sbctl and on NixOS with Lanzaboote or Limine.

There are still many unsolved problems in hardware security, so this is definitely not solved today. But I don’t see why it should be impossible. The EU could push the industry toward attestation models that respect user ownership instead of locking everything behind vendor-controlled software.

楼层
summm 2026/07/02 18:11
我认为 Meneth 基本上是正确的，但有点不精确：只要用户可以自由决定和更改完整性参考值，启动完整性本身就是对用户的尊重，而不会对其系统的后续有用性产生影响。但认证（基于启动完整性）作为一项原则直接与“尊重用户所有权”相矛盾。 这不是技术可行性的问题，而是原则性的问题。证明是针对用户的。证明的主要目的是确保本地用户可以被迫使用特定的软件（并且不能同时运行其他可能影响该特定软件的软件）来访问某个远程服务。 根据定义，远程证明意味着“供应商锁定”，即供应商及其合同伙伴锁定和控制设备的软件。 因此，完全尊重用户的认证模型根本不存在。
I think Meneth was basically correct but a bit imprecise: Boot integrity itself is only user respectful as long as the user can freely decide and change the integrity reference values, without consequences to the latter usefulness of their system. But attestation (based on boot integrity) as a principle directly contradicts "respect user ownership". This is not a question of technical feasibility, but of principle. Attestation is directed against users. The main purpose of attestation is to make sure that the local user can be forced to use a specific software (and not run other software at the same time that could influence this specific software) to access a certain remote service. Remote attestation means by definition "vendor lock", in that the vendor and their contractual partners lock and control the device's software. Therefore an attestation model that fully respects the user simply cannot exist.
楼层
ralferoo 2026/06/30 20:28
确切地说，我不确定硬件认证能为政府带来什么好处。当然，对于客户来说，他们可以相信自己的密钥在设备上是安全的，这可能很有用，但这有点没有抓住要点。

它实际上应该是一个定义标准协议的开源规范，但设备只需使用政府 API 知道代表您的密钥来签署它知道来自可信来源的请求（因此可能由政府的密钥签名）。

因此，我设想像政府门户之类的东西可以让您添加一堆公钥，每个设备一个，并共享自己的公钥，可用于验证任何请求。想要验证您身份的机构可以请求您的公钥，并向政府 API 请求其传回给您的质询令牌。您可以验证质询令牌是否由您信任的密钥签名，您可以对质询进行签名并将其返回给应用程序，应用程序可以将其传递回政府 API，然后政府可以授予对其请求的任何信息子集的访问权限（并且质询密钥可以包含足够的信息，以便签名应用程序提出有意义的请求）。

就协议而言非常简单。只有政府需要存储您的任何私人数据。如果应用程序只需要知道您是否达到足够的年龄，那么这就是它获得的所有信息。如果您丢失了设备，您可以轻松撤销密钥并添加新密钥。

当然，手机上的特定实现可能需要使用硬件证明来保证其密钥的安全，但没有理由必须强制执行。设计良好的公钥系统应该足以让实现保护其密钥，同时在需要时提供一种简单的方法来替换密钥。

Exactly, I'm not sure what benefits hardware attestation offers to the government. Sure, it's potentially useful for the customer that they can trust their keys are secure on their device, but it kind of misses the point.

It should really be an open-source specification that defines a standard protocol, but where the device just signs a request that it knows has come from a trusted source (so maybe signed by the government's key) with a key that the government's API knows that represents you.

So, I'd envisage something like government portal lets you add a bunch of public keys, one for each device, and shares a public key of its own that can be used to verify any requests. Something that wants to verify your identity can request your public key, and ask the government API for a challenge token which it passed back to you. You can verify the challenge token is signed by the key you trust, you can sign the challenge and return it to the app, which can pass it back to the government API which can then grant access to whatever subset of information they requested (and the challenge key can include enough information for the signing app to present a meaningful request).

Very simple in terms of protocol. Only the government needs to store any of your private data. If an application just needs to know if you are of a sufficient age or not, that's all the information it gets. If you lose your device you can easily revoke your keys and add new ones.

Sure, a specific implementation on a phone might want to use hardware attestation in order to keep its keys safe, but there's no reason that it has to be mandated. A well designed public key system should be sufficient leaving the implementation to safeguard its keys, while providing a simple way to replace keys if needed.

楼层
ulrikrasmussen 2026/06/30 21:11
我认为这些系统需要设备绑定密钥的原因是政府担心容易批量生产的伪造年龄证书。使用软件密钥，您可以获得年龄证书，该证书可以立即复制到大量设备，使用硬件密钥，政府知道该证书与单个物理单元绑定。
I think the reason these systems require device bound keys is because the government is concerned with easily mass-produced forged age certificates. With software keys you can get an age certificate which can be copied instantly to a large number of devices, with hardware keys the government knows that the certificate is tied to a single physical unit.
楼层
KoolKat23 2026/06/30 21:19
再说一次，在这一点上，他们太过分了，年龄之门不应该是一座坚不可摧的堡垒（尽管首先存在它们是否应该存在的问题）。

如果设备已获得 root 权限，则设备上的成人帐户应该会收到通知，实际上不再处于儿童模式。如果你愿意的话，可以对两台设备上的警告疯狂，因为他们当时已经选择了加入。

Again, at this point, they're taking things too far,age gates shouldn't need to be an impenetrable fortress (notwithstanding the question of whether they should exist in the first place).

It should simply be the adult account on the device is notified if the device is rooted, effectively no longer in child mode. Go crazy with the warnings on both devices if you want as they've opted in at that point.

楼层
tomega2134 2026/07/01 00:17
该欧盟协议是否太弱以至于无法抵御这种攻击，即是否未检测或防止重复年龄证书的使用？
Is this EU protocol so weak that it cannot withstand this attack, i.e. is duplicate age certificate use not detected or prevented?
楼层
ulrikrasmussen 2026/07/01 01:45
你无法真正阻止这种情况，除非你设计一个本质上是为了跟踪人员的系统，例如在每次凭证验证时给发行人打电话。目前正在部署的系统基于发行者向设备绑定的一次性密钥发放批量的一次性凭证令牌，这从好的方面来说意味着合谋验证者无法使用年龄凭证作为 cookie 来跟踪人员。但它仍然容易受到验证者和发行者串通的攻击，因为发行者可以对代币进行去匿名化（它知道它们以及它们与用户身份的链接）。此方案还意味着，如果颁发令牌的密钥未绑定设备，则将年龄凭证复制给其他人是很简单的。

据我所知，即使是更复杂的 ZKP 方案仍然依赖设备绑定密钥来防止重复。

You can't really prevent that unless you design a system which is inherently designed to track people, e.g. by phoning home to the issuer on each credential verification. The system being deployed right now is based on the issuer issuing batches of single-use credential tokens to device-bound single-use keys, which on the plus side means that colluding verifiers cannot use age credentials as cookies to track people. It is still vulnerable to colluding verifiers and issuers though, because the issuer can de-anonymize the tokens (it knows them and their linking to the identity of the user). This scheme also means that if the keys that the tokens are issued to are not device bound, then it is trivial to copy the age credentials to someone else.

To my knowledge, even more sophisticated ZKP schemes still rely on device bound keys to protect against duplication.

Borealid 2026/06/30 22:51

问题：如果您没有硬件或操作环境的证明，如何确保密钥是设备绑定的？

> it just requires a few cryptographic primitives and a set of device-bound keys

Question: how do you make sure the keys are device-bound if you have no attestation about the hardware or operating environment?

楼层
modin 2026/06/30 22:05
我明白你的观点，即言论与我们在生产中实际看到的情况之间存在脱节。也许“回归”是一个很强烈的词，但恕我直言，我倾向于将其视为一种非常缓慢且不平衡的演变。

I see your point about the disconnect between the rhetoric and what we actually see in production. Perhaps "regression" is a strong word, though, IMHO I tend to see it as a very slow and uneven evolution.

Even if the pace is frustrating, there are still pockets of genuine open-source adoption in the European public sector. For example, we're seeing projects like Germany's OpenDesk or various municipalities moving toward Nextcloud and other sovereign cloud solutions.

The EU Open Source Strategy[0] was announced just under a month ago and it specifically mentions the EU Digital Identity ecosystem, including the European Digital Identity Wallet (EUDI Wallet) mentioned in the article. I agree with OOP that the requirement of an Apple or Google phone goes against these ambitions, and I will contact my elected representatives.

[0]: https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/open-sourc...

> 为了确保用户可以信任电子钱包解决方案，电子钱包提供商最好通过相关操作系统（例如 Android、iOS）的官方应用商店提供经过认证的电子钱包解决方案以供安装。这允许设备的操作系统执行有关应用程序真实性的相关检查。

Yes, and there is an open source spec [0] that doesn’t require Google/iOS Attestation but “preferably” providers will make their wallet app available on App Stores [1]:

> To ensure that the User can trust the Wallet Solution, Wallet Providers preferably make their certified Wallet Solutions available for installation via the official app store of the relevant operating system (e.g., Android, iOS). This allows the operating system of the device to perform relevant checks regarding the authenticity of the app.

Of course the chances of any important business implementing a side channel option is effectively zero. Maybe some government agencies will offer the option though.

p2detar 2026/06/30 22:14
显然不是。这就是技术人员忘记的事情：大量欧洲人根本不关心数字主权或开源。天啊，人们去移动运营商商店，把他们解锁的手机交给顾问，让他们安装或删除软件。您希望他们安装 GrapheneOS 或管理已取得 root 权限的设备吗？这一点都不好笑。

唯一的短期解决方案是加强监管和更加集中于欧盟的解决方案，但当然，这只有在下一场聊天控制戏剧发生之前才可以。

从长远来看，实际上我们需要单一的欧洲股票市场，以及一种向任何成员国的欧洲公司提供资金的方式，以便在全球范围内具有竞争力，而不会不断受到每个成员国官僚机构的限制。

Clearly it isn’t. This is what techies forget: The mass amount of Europeans don’t give 2 shits about digital sovereignty or open source. Christ, people go to mobile operator shops and give their unlocked phones to consultants to install or remove software for them. You want them to install GrapheneOS or manage a rooted device? That ain’t even funny.

The only short-term solution is more regulation and more EU-centralized solutions, but of course this is only ok until the next chat-control drama.

Long term, in practice we need single European stock market and a way to provide funding to European companies from any member state, so to be competitive globally without being constantly restricted by every member state’s bureaucracy.

wqaatwt 2026/06/30 23:34
> 我们需要单一的欧洲股票市场

仅纽约就有两家证券交易所。我不认为在伦敦、巴黎和法兰克福上市的公司（无论如何，它们通常都会在几家上市）是真正的问题。

尤其是现在股市已经是最后阶段了，成长型科技公司的大部分资金都是私募基金、风险投资等。

> we need single European stock market

New York alone has 2 stock exchanges. I don’t think that companies being listed in London, Paris and Frankfurt (they generally are listed on several anyway) is the actual issue.

Especially these days the stock market is the very final stage anyway, most funding for growing tech companies is private funds, vc etc.

haagch 2026/07/01 16:42

https://nlnet.nl/project/postmarketOS/

https://nlnet.nl/project/MobileSettings

https://nlnet.nl/project/pmOS-23-24

https://nlnet.nl/project/postmarketOS-daemons

https://nlnet.nl/project/pmOS-25-26

我还感觉到，关于这笔资金的报道和讨论如此之少，这一事实反映了人们对替代方案的实际兴趣水平。

The EU's NGI Zero in cooperation with NLNet has been funding quite a bit of postmarketOS

https://nlnet.nl/project/postmarketOS/

https://nlnet.nl/project/MobileSettings

https://nlnet.nl/project/pmOS-23-24

https://nlnet.nl/project/postmarketOS-daemons

https://nlnet.nl/project/pmOS-25-26

I think if NGI Zero had been around when Meego was killed, we might be in a better situation now.

I also get the feeling, that the fact that there is so little reported and discussed about this funding reflects the actual level of interest in alternatives.

美国可以在 5 分钟内打电话给奥地利，无需提供举证责任，就可以吊销主权国家元首的领空许可，飞机着陆后立即遭到猛烈打击，因为飞机上可能有人（他没有），他唯一真正的罪行是揭露他们根本上违宪的违法行为，让美国难堪。

瑞典的检察官也是如此；接到一通电话后，美国收到的不是指控（因为这在瑞典实际上属于官方不当行为），而是检察官的一份足够的官方声明，到那天晚上，“阿桑奇”和“强奸”这​​两个词就同时出现在世界各地的头条新闻中。

欧洲国家总的来说是美国的走狗。很伤心。 然后美国总统却反过来在背后捅他们一刀，威胁入侵和吞并，或者完全无视北约成员国的基本义务。

我真不知道欧洲人玩美国的愚蠢游戏到底在想什么。 正如我们一次又一次看到的那样，它不会得到实物回报。

The US can call Austria in 5 minutes and with no burden of proof get the airspace permit for a head of sovereign state revoked and the plane swatted instantly upon landing, because someone might have been on board (he wasn’t) whose only real crime was embarrassing the USA by exposing their fundamentally unconstitutional lawbreaking.

Same goes with the prosecutors in Sweden; a phone call and the US got, not charges (as that would actually be official misconduct in Sweden), but enough of an official statement from a prosecutor to get the words “Assange” and “rape” in headlines together around the world by that evening.

European countries are, by and large, lapdogs of the USA. It’s sad. And then the US president turns around and stabs them in the back by threatening invasion and annexation, or complete disregard for the fundamental obligations of NATO members.

I really don’t know what the fuck the Europeans are thinking by playing the US’s stupid games. As we see time and time again, it won’t be repaid in kind.

inglor_cz 2026/06/30 21:16

84 thousand personnel (of which maybe 20 per cent are actual combat troops, given the standard tooth-to-tail ratios of modern mechanized armies) could perhaps occupy Denmark on a good day. For a continent the size and population of Europe, this is not a dominant force by any means.

Putin has about 700 000 personnel in Ukraine right now and isn't making any progress. Barbarossa took about 3 million personnel to start.

这将需要 100 年的时间，并且需要政府强制要求对美国每一项关键技术服务和公司进行极其昂贵的重新实施。

Europe will never have digital sovereignty from the US.

It will take 100 years and an extremely expensive, government-mandated reimplementation of every critical US tech service and company.

No EU country is putting up budget for this, and no private enterprise is going to do it because building a worse version of AWS just so that it is "European" makes no financial sense and would most likely just fail anyway.

楼层
Tangurena2 2026/06/30 22:10
它不是 只是 王牌。 《云法案》基本上赋予华盛顿随意/心血来潮关闭任何美国公司运营的任何服务器的权力和能力。

维基百科页面仅讨论（可以是外国）服务器上存储的数据，而不考虑该服务器所在国家/地区的法律。它忽略了法规中联邦政府基本上可以“关闭”该服务器的部分。和 那 这是欧盟感到恐慌的部分。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CLOUD_Act

It isn't just Trump. The CLOUD Act basically gives Washington the power and ability to turn off any server operated by any US company at will/whim.

The Wikipedia page only talks about stored data on (optionally foreign) servers without any sort of regard for the laws of the country where that server is located. It ignores the part of the statute where the feds can basically "turn off" that server. And that is the part that the EU is panicking over.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CLOUD_Act

Escapado 2026/06/30 19:28
我同意这个前提，但感觉这与钱无关。德国人使用 WhatsApp、Instagram、Gmail、MS Office 和 Windows，并不是因为没有其他选择，而是因为他们不知道或不愿意更换。众所周知，即使人们能在另一方获得更多利益，也很难说服他们转换平台。我妈妈不想接触除 Outlook 之外的任何电子邮件客户端，她除了阅读和偶尔回复单一电子邮件之外什么也不做，而且她只需要电子邮件客户端的最基本功能。当 Windows 界面再次发生变化时，我的一半家人都会感到恐慌。最近，在我的相当技术化的朋友圈中，更换信使的想法导致了为期多天的讨论，但没有真正的结果，主要是因为有些人只是不想与两个信使打交道，而他们的朋友和家人仍然不相信。我们已经拥有来自欧洲提供商的社交媒体、托管、电子邮件、操作系统、即时通讯工具等。人们只是不想改变。
I agree with the premise but have the feeling that it’s less about the money. People here in Germany use WhatsApp and Instagram and Gmail and MS Office and Windows not because there are no alternatives but because they either don’t know or don’t care to switch. People are notoriously difficult to convince to switch platforms even if they‘d get more benefits on the other side. My mom does not want to touch any email client besides outlook and she does nothing but read and very occasionally reply to singular emails and she requires only the barest functionality of an email client. Half of my family gets a panic attack when the windows interface changes again. The idea of switching messengers recently in my rather tech sawy circle of friends has resulted in a multi day discussion with no real outcome mainly because some just don’t want to deal with two messengers while their friends and family remain unconvinced. We already have social media, hosting, email, operating systems, messengers and the likes from European providers. People just don’t want to switch.
楼层
mft_ 2026/06/30 19:51
呃，它没有你描述的那么固定。

如果有更高级别的授权或激励来转换，人们绝对会这样做 - 例如，如果政府决定 很多 放弃一个操作系统或平台。 [0]。这可能会产生巨大的影响，因为每个想要与该政府有效沟通的人都需要确保 他们 兼容——随着时间的推移，这可能会推动替代技术的采用。

然而，在我看来，最大的挑战是 MS Office——尽管人们喜欢提及 FOSS Office 的替代品，但在主流公司采用它们之前，仍然有一个巨大的差距需要跨越。 （换句话来说，没有人会因为选择 Microsoft Office 而被解雇。）

除此之外，在您讨论的更“个人”的层面上，情况比您描述的更加多样化。 有些人年迈的父母绝对可以而且确实切换到不同的电子邮件客户端或浏览器。 一些朋友组可以并且确实会切换通讯平台 - 我的个人通讯现在在 Whatsapp（默认）和 Signal 之间分配大约 80:20。 （只有少数坚定的人决定改变，其他人也纷纷效仿。）

> 我们已经拥有来自欧洲提供商的社交媒体、托管、电子邮件、操作系统、即时通讯工具等。

是的，但他们不是 真的 具有竞争力，因为它们目前并不是每个人第一次获得计算机时默认使用的简单/免费/市场良好/流行的选项，或者他们的朋友已经在使用。 这只是网络效应和惯性。

如果减少对美国的依赖的需要继续成为人们关注的焦点，这种情况就可以而且将会改变。 （请注意，这主要是由特朗普政府的行为驱动的；下一任总统可能会治愈其中许多伤口，而我们的欧洲政客将让人们去关心其他事情。）

[0] https://www.rfi.fr/en/france/20260417-france-to-remove-windo...

Eh, it's less fixed than you describe.

If there is a higher level mandate or incentive to switch, people absolutely will - for example, if a government decides en masse to switch away from one OS or platform. [0]. This will likely be hugely influential, as then everyone who wants to communicate effectively with that government needs to make sure that they are compatible - which will likely drive adoption of the alternate technologies over time.

However, IMO the big challenge is MS Office - as much as people like to mention the FOSS Office alternatives, there's still a huge gap to cross before mainstream companies will adopt them. (To paraphrase, no-one gets fired for choosing Microsoft Office.)

Beyond this, on the more 'personal' level you discuss, the picture is more varied than you describe. Some people's elderly parents absolutely can and do switch to different email clients or browsers. Some groups of friends can and do switch messenger platforms - my personal comms are now split roughly 80:20 between Whatsapp (the default) and Signal. (It just took a determined minority deciding to switch, and the others followed.)

> We already have social media, hosting, email, operating systems, messengers and the likes from European providers.

Yes, but they aren't really competitive, as they currently aren't the easy/free/well-marketed/popular options that everyone defaults to when they first get a computer, or that their friends are already using. It's just network effect and inertia.

This can and will change if the need for a reduced dependence on the US continues to be front and center of people's minds. (Note this is mostly driven by the Trump administration's behaviour; the next president could probably heal many of these wounds and our European politicians will move one to caring about something else.)

[0] https://www.rfi.fr/en/france/20260417-france-to-remove-windo...

楼层
phyzix5761 2026/06/30 19:07
法规造成垄断。即使监管旨在遏制巨头的控制，规模较小的企业通常也无力承担并失去市场份额。这实际上是商学院作为竞争优势策略教授的。公司游说政府实施看似对他们不利的法律，但实际上创造了一个不公平的竞争环境，由于实施成本较高，他们可以获得市场份额。
Regulations create monopolies. Even when regulations are aimed at curbing the control of giants, smaller players usually can't afford them and lose market share. This is actually taught as a competitive advantage strategy in business school. Corporations lobby the government to implement laws that seem to hurt them but in actuality create an uneven playing field where marketshare becomes available due to the higher implementation cost.
楼层
podgorniy 2026/06/30 19:19
如果根本没有监管，垄断难道不是我们默认的结局吗？

是的，并非所有监管都会摧毁垄断，但监管是唯一可以打破垄断的因素。

Aren't monopolies is what we end up by default if have no regulation at all?

And yes, not every regulation destroys monopoly, but regulation is the only thing that could break one.

楼层
Arkhaine_kupo 2026/06/30 19:25
> 如果根本没有监管，垄断难道不是我们默认的结局吗？

不会。只有当商品没有弹性、进入市场的成本很高，或者市场可以创造一条无法逾越的护城河时，垄断才是不可避免的。

许多市场即使在零监管的情况下也没有这样的问题，但可能会出现二阶问题，例如公司生产不安全的产品。

但一般来说，监管几乎总是会无意中提高进入市场的成本。如果制定一个新的规定，食品需要安全，那么企业需要支付安全检查费用，这是一个小自制菜谱可能无法承担的（举一个简单的例子）。

与此同时，由于供应链的非弹性部分（如土地）或难以逾越的护城河（如获得美国资本），我们现在拥有超级大公司。在这种情况下，FCC 应该打破垄断，因为当前的市场不是迎合最终用户和消费者，而是迎合所有者，这就是为什么股市一直处于永不停歇的牛市之中。

> Aren't monopolies is what we end up by default if have no regulation at all?

No. Monopolies are only inevitable if the goods aren't elastic, if there is a large cost of entry into the market, or if its a market you can create a moat that is unsurmountable.

Many markets don't have that even with 0 regulation, but might have second order problems like firms creating unsafe products for example.

But in general regulations almost always even unindentedly raise the cost to enter the market. If you make a new regulation that food needs to be safe, then the company needs to pay a safety inspection that a small home-made recipe might not be able to afford (to give a simple example).

At the same time, we now have uber large corporations due to non elastic parts of supply chain (like land) or moats that are insurmountable (like access to US capital). In which case, the FCC should break up monopolies as the current market is not catering to end users and consumers but to owners, which is why the Stock market has been in a never ending bull run.

楼层
kalaksi 2026/06/30 21:17
难道大公司不是也经常以多种方式从规模中受益，从而让新来者越来越难以竞争吗？如果一个新来者确实能站稳脚跟，它可能会被收购。
Don't bigger companies also often benefit from scale in multiple ways so it gets harder and harder for newcomers to compete? And if a newcomer does manage to get a foothold, it might get bought.
楼层
Arkhaine_kupo 2026/06/30 21:27
> 大公司不是也经常以多种方式从规模中受益，因此新来者的竞争变得越来越困难吗？

这是护城河和垄断发生的方式之一。例如，如果你是唯一拥有织布机的人，而其他人都必须手工制作毛衣，那么你可以将它们制造得很便宜，以至于他们不得不关闭。

在某些市场中，存在可以从规模中获益的方式，而在另一些市场中则存在缺点。在许多情况下，这些优势的存在只是由于监管或监管缺失。

例如，公司可能拥有优势的方式是在更便宜的国家进行制造，但这之所以有效，是因为这些工人的权利较少，而且运输成本没有得到适当的征税。对航运征收碳税将使中国制造业的价格与许多欧洲国家相当。但如果你让他们用原油船污染海洋，那么他们的制造能力和廉价的劳动力成本将抵消运输成本并摧毁一个新来者。

这些都是非常基本的例子，它们都需要细微差别，但希望它有助于更​​多地解释它。

另一个例子是餐馆，以前作为连锁店有一些优势，但你仍然会不断看到夫妻店竞争甚至获胜。但随着租金价格不断上涨（租赁土地的非弹性市场），突然间，规模优势开始战胜食品和服务较差的劣势。

> Don't bigger companies also often benefit from scale in multiple ways so it gets harder and harder for newcomers to compete?

That is one of the ways a Moat can happen and a monopoly can occur. For example if you were the only person with a loom and everyone else had to make jumpers by hand, you could make them so cheap they would have to close down.

In some markets those ways you can benefit from scale exist, in others there are drawbacks. In many cases those advantages only exist due to either regulation or lac thereof.

For example ways companies might have an advantage is by manufacturing in cheaper countries, but that only works because those workers have less rights and the cost of transporting is not properly taxed. Carbon taxes on shipping would make manufacturing in China pretty comparatively priced to many european countries. But if you let them contaminate the ocean with crude oil boats, then their manufacturing prowess and cheaper labour cost will offset the shipping cost and destroy a newcomer.

These are very basic examples and they all require nuance but hope it helps to explain it a bit more.

Another example is restaurants, you used to have some advantages from being a chain, but you would still constantly see mom and pop joints compete and even win. But as rent prices keep increasing (the non elastic market of the ground under the lease), suddenly the advantages of scale start beating the disadvantages of worse food and service.

楼层
Tangurena2 2026/06/30 22:17
MS 进行了大量游说，阻止欧洲政府尝试迁移到 Linux 和/或 OpenDocument。

Groklaw 是一个由律师助理创建的网站，旨在了解、解释和报道 SCO 诉讼 - 谁受益以及他们如何受益。它最终扩展到欧盟针对 Microsoft 和 OpenDocument 的反垄断行动（以及 OpenOffice 如何被创建为特洛伊木马来摧毁 OpenDocument）。

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groklaw

MS did a lot of lobbying to prevent European governments from trying to migrate to Linux and/or OpenDocument.

Groklaw was a website that was started by a paralegal to try to understand, explain and report on the SCO lawsuit - who benefited and how they benefited. It ended up expanding into the EU anti-trust action against Microsoft and OpenDocument (and how OpenOffice was created as a trojan horse to defang OpenDocument).

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groklaw

KoolKat23 2026/06/30 19:35
信息总是不完美的，完美的市场并不存在，因此总是需要监管来遏制垄断等过度行为。即使我们拥有完美的信息，人类仍然是非理性的。这是生命和宇宙的一个简单事实。
There is always imperfect information, there is no such thing as a perfect market and as a result regulation will always be needed to curb the excesses such as monopoly. Even if we had perfect information, humans remain irrational. This is a simple fact of life and the universe.

maybewhenthesun 2026/06/30 19:45
我不这么认为。因为关于弹性市场和垄断的理论确实带有浓厚的“球形无摩擦牛”气味。他们在这里被当作福音。因此，虽然将某人定性为 120 岁可能有点人身攻击，但 做 简洁地指出问题并因此添加信息。
I don't think so. Because the theories about elastic markets and monopolies do have a high 'spherical frictionless cow` smell. And they are posed here as gospel. So while it might be a bit of an ad hominem to frame someone as a 120 year old it does succinctly point out a problem and hence adds information.

Things "having a smell" is not an argument, and they weren't posed as gospel.

hypeatei 2026/06/30 20:07
> 它们被摆在这里作为福音

> And they are posed here as gospel

The same could be said for people who suggest regulation for every problem that comes up, even for problems that were caused by regulation. Maybe we have our blindspots, but the "regulate everything" crowd is much louder and more prevalent on HN than the free market absolutists.

Arkhaine_kupo 2026/06/30 20:03
我的意思是，有没有任何经验证据证明这些基本假设是错误的？

在经济学中，最简单的部分是创建模型，最困难的部分是看到它与现实相冲突。但垄断的基础似乎受到了相当彻底的考验。你们现在遇到的最大问题是切斯特顿栅栏的问题。很难知道哪些法律法规是安全性、平等性和经济绩效的，而且由于多年来法律凌驾于其他法律之上，这些法律法规只会制造摩擦而没有任何好处

I mean, has there any empirical evidence disproven any of those base assumptions?

In econ the easiest part is to create a model, the hardest part is seeing it crash against reality. But the basis of monopolies seems to be pretty thoroughly tested. The biggest issues you have now are Chesterton Fence's. Were its hard to know what laws and regulations are therefore safety, parity and economic performance and ones are only creating friction with no benefit due to years of laws being put on top of other laws

throwwwll 2026/06/30 20:27
我从来都不是友善的，但我承认我没有更好的文学手段来简洁地表达我对你有缺陷的立场的看法，说得好听一些。

我支持监管，对我来说监管是打破垄断、防止公地悲剧、制定必要的质量检查、禁止强迫劳动。我反对监管，比如聊天控制、任何事情 100% 关税、禁止周五 17:00 以后工作以及完全禁止周末工作。 <为什么我还要指出这一点？

这个世界上没有任何细微差别，人们（传教的书呆子类型）在情感上依附于诡辩，即阐明的经济理论，而完全无视常识。树木不见森林。

简而言之——操美国。

I have never been nice, but I admit I have no better literary device to concisely express my sentiment towards your flawed position, to put it nicely.

I am pro-regulation, where regulation for me is busting monopolies, preventing tragedy of commons, setting necessary quality checks, forbidding forced labor. I am against regulation, when it's chat control, 100% tarrifs on whatever, forbidding working on Friday past 17:00 and completely on Weekends. < Why do I even have to point this out?

There's no nuance remaining in this world, people (the proselytizing nerd types) emotionally attach themselves to sophistry that is the spelled-out economic theories, while completely disregarding common sense. Missing forest for trees.

In short - fuck America.

Chu4eeno 2026/06/30 19:26
欧洲有没有（成功）打破垄断的例子？或者你认为监管会阻止它们的形成？
Are there any examples of monopolies being (successfully) broken up in Europe? Or do you posit that regulation stop them from forming?
楼层
doikor 2026/06/30 19:42
二战前的欧洲充满了（国家支持的）卡特尔和垄断。这些大部分已被拆除。

其中很多都是国际性的。只需阅读“卡特尔资本主义”即可。

https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/enterprise-and-socie...

欧洲钢铁和煤炭共同体（欧盟的前身）也参与了阻止这些行为的努力。总的来说，这是欧盟自成立以来一直参与的事情，反对垄断的最佳行动就是从一开始就不让垄断形成（为什么大多数发达国家的垄断很少。尽管现在这种情况似乎正在慢慢改变）

Pre WW2 Europe was full of (state backed) cartels and monopolies. These were dismantled for the most part.

A lot of these were international. Just read up on "Cartel capitalism".

https://www.cambridge.org/core/journals/enterprise-and-socie...

The European Steel and Coal Community (precursor of the EU) was also involved in the effort to stop these. In general this has been something the EU has been involved in since its inception and the best action against monopolies is to not let them form in the first place (why there is so few of them in general in most developed countries. Though that is now slowly changing it seems)

Look into the mechanisms being worked on to create competition in rail operators (which has been opening the markets to competitor rail operators)

slopinthebag 2026/07/01 01:08
不，事实上，大多数垄断发生在受到严格监管的市场，而不受监管的市场实际上总是自由的。

请记住，仅仅拥有较大的市场份额并不构成垄断，能够收取垄断价格才是垄断。

No, in fact most monopolies occur in heavily regulated markets, where unregulated markets are virtually always free.

Keep in mind that just having a big market share isn’t a monopoly, being able to charge monopoly prices is.

楼层
braiamp 2026/06/30 20:14
> 法规造成垄断。即使监管旨在遏制巨头的控制，规模较小的企业通常也无力承担并失去市场份额。这实际上是商学院作为竞争优势策略教授的。公司游说政府实施看似对他们不利的法律，但实际上创造了一个不公平的竞争环境，由于实施成本较高，他们可以获得市场份额。

保证垄断的唯一方法是完全缺乏监管。众所周知，每一个“自由”市场都会因为1%法则而趋于垄断。监管是真正保证自由市场的唯一途径，因为完美的自由市场只存在于抽象中，而不是现实中。有时，自由市场是错误的解决方案，你需要一个受监管的垄断，而拥有身份才是最好的解决方案。为什么？因为身份对于个人来说是独一无二的。一个人（理论上）必须只有一种身份，并且除了非常极端且通常有据可查的例外情况外，这种身份不会改变。国家必须为身份提供一种良好的方式，如果小国没有资源，那么大国应该为所有人提供资源。此外，它消除了国家间的不兼容性，同时将私人利益排除在外。

国家应该拥有证明任何人身份的唯一垄断权。因为他们是唯一不受市场状况影响的。这就是在这一主题上取得进展的国家的实际工作方式。如果个别国家无法达成共同解决方案，那么集体必须这样做。该集体在这里失败了，因为它推荐了一种私人解决方案，而不是强制要求欧洲解决方案。私营部门不得决定证明什么或如何证明身份，因为私营部门有其追求利润的议程，国家必须评估解决方案，但由各州来管理和实施它们。

市场解决方案有很多好处，但这不是其中之一。

> Regulations create monopolies. Even when regulations are aimed at curbing the control of giants, smaller players usually can't afford them and lose market share. This is actually taught as a competitive advantage strategy in business school. Corporations lobby the government to implement laws that seem to hurt them but in actuality create an uneven playing field where marketshare becomes available due to the higher implementation cost.

The only way to guarantee a monopoly is to have a total lack of regulation. It's known that every "free" market will tend towards monopoly due the 1% law. Regulations are the only way to actually guarantee free markets because perfect free markets only exists in abstract, not in reality. Sometimes, a free market is the wrong solution and you need a regulated monopoly instead and with identity that's the best solution. Why? Because identity is unique to the individual. A individual must (in theory) only have one identity and with very extreme and usually well documented exceptions, such identity doesn't change. The state is the one that must provide a good way for identity and if smaller countries doesn't have the resources, then big countries should provide for all. Also, it removes incompatibility inter-countries while keeping private interests out.

The state should have the sole monopoly on attesting to anyone identity. Because they are the only ones that are not affected by market conditions. This is how countries that have advanced in this topic actually work. If individual states can't reach a common solution, then the collective must do so. The collective failed here because it recommended a private solution rather than mandated a european one. Private sector must not dictate what or how identity is attested, because the private sector has it's profit pursuing agenda, state must evaluate solutions but it's up to the states to run them and implement them.

Market solutions are good for several things, this isn't one of them.

楼层
thibaut_barrere 2026/06/30 19:26
除非法规明确规定如何处理现有人员和新人。其中一个例子是 MMTIS（多模式乘客信息），它明确将创新和新参与者作为目标。还有其他类似的例子。
Unless regulations explicitely incorporate how to handle incumbents & newcomers. One instance of that is MMTIS (multi modal passenger information), which explicitly states innovation and new players as a goal. There are other similar examples.
楼层
Escapado 2026/06/30 19:20
我的直觉是，这不一定是真的，但在实践中可能经常是正确的，但也许在这个问题上受过更多教育的人可以对此发表看法。它还必须取决于相关监管的成本。由于在许多领域，法规绝对至关重要，例如，我们的建筑物不会倒塌，我们的食物保持无毒，我们购买的药物不是俄罗斯轮盘赌的药理学等价物，因此目标应该是优化法规的成本绩效。
My intuition is that this is not necessarily true, but probably often true in practice but perhaps someone more educated on the matter can speak on that. It must also depend on the expensiveness of the regulation in question. Since in tons of areas regulations are absolutely vital so that for example our buildings don’t collapse, our food remains non-toxic and the medicine we buy is not the pharmacological equivalent to russian roulette the goal should then be to optimise the cost performance of regulations.
